experiment_summary.description: Comparison of Probabilistic vs Round-Robin scheduling algorithms

comparison.winner: tie (both algorithms perform similarly at low load)
comparison.note: At low load (5 QPS), both algorithms achieve 100% success with similar latency

round_robin.reason: Queue congestion - algorithm doesn't consider queue depth or instance load
notes: Round-robin distributes requests evenly regardless of instance state
At 20 QPS with 1:5:20 runtime ratios, slow model queues build up exponentially
Test times out after 600 seconds with tasks stuck in queue

comparison.reason: Round-robin fails completely at high load while probabilistic maintains 100% success
comparison.key_insight: Probabilistic scheduling considers queue depth and predicted latency for load balancing

conclusions: Probabilistic scheduling is significantly more robust under high load
Round-robin works adequately at low load but fails at capacity limits
The 4x load increase (5 QPS -> 20 QPS) exposes round-robin's weakness
Probabilistic scheduling maintains consistent latency even at high load
For production use with variable load, probabilistic scheduling is recommended
